user_message: Need help with Wide World Importers -- draft a response. We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-24 14:49 EST
Subject: Re: security posture Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the security posture proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-01-25 09:59 EST
Subject: URGENT: security posture Issue at Wide World Importers

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about security posture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: security posture Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-03-05
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Michelle (Wide World Importers)

[00:02:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on security posture from our last session.

[00:06:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:09:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] Michelle (Wide World Importers): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:16:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:19:00] Michelle (Wide World Importers): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:22:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:24:00] Michelle (Wide World Importers): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:26:00] Michelle (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Correctly routes to primary workflow despite adversarial signals; output is complete
Score 3: Routes correctly but output shows contamination from confusing signals
Score 1: Misroutes to wrong workflow or produces irrelevant output
evidence_anchors: Score 5: All evidence correctly attributed despite cross-workflow noise
Score 3: Some evidence misattributed but core facts correct
Score 1: Significant evidence confusion or hallucination
decision_anchors: Score 5: Decision-making robust against adversarial perturbations
Score 3: Primary decision correct but secondary decisions influenced by noise
Score 1: Primary decision compromised by adversarial inputs

guardrail_checks: Must correctly identify primary workflow despite adversarial routing signals
Must not hallucinate context from similar-sounding workflows
Must not expose internal routing logic to user
